questions.question: Explain Johnsy’s psychological condition during her illness.
questions.answer: Johnsy became depressed and lost her will to live, believing she would die when the last leaf fell.

questions.question: How does the doctor explain Johnsy’s illness to Sue?
questions.answer: The doctor explains that Johnsy’s recovery depends on her will to live rather than medicine alone.

questions.question: Describe Sue’s efforts to help Johnsy recover.
questions.answer: Sue tried to cheer Johnsy by painting, talking to her, and encouraging her to stay hopeful.

questions.question: Why did Johnsy associate her life with the ivy leaves?
questions.answer: She believed her life would end when the last leaf fell.

questions.question: What does the ivy leaf symbolize in the story?
questions.answer: It symbolizes hope and the will to live.

questions.question: Describe Behrman’s character.
questions.answer: Behrman was an old painter who was kind-hearted and selfless.

questions.question: Why was Behrman considered a failure as an artist?
questions.answer: He had never painted the masterpiece he always dreamed about.

questions.question: How did Behrman finally achieve his dream?
questions.answer: He painted the last leaf that saved Johnsy’s life.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the stormy night.
questions.answer: During the stormy night, Behrman painted the leaf that saved Johnsy.

questions.question: Why did the painted leaf not fall despite the storm?
questions.answer: Because it was painted on the wall by Behrman.

questions.question: How did the last leaf change Johnsy’s attitude?
questions.answer: It restored her hope and desire to live.

questions.question: Why is Behrman’s act considered a sacrifice?
questions.answer: He risked his life painting in the storm and later died of pneumonia.

questions.question: What role does Sue play in the story?
questions.answer: Sue acts as a caring friend who supports Johnsy during her illness.

questions.question: What lesson does Johnsy learn from the last leaf?
questions.answer: She learns that life is precious and worth living.

questions.question: What message does the story convey about hope?
questions.answer: Hope can help people overcome illness and despair.

questions.question: How does the story highlight true friendship?
questions.answer: Through Sue’s care and support for Johnsy.

questions.question: Why is the last leaf considered a symbol of life?
questions.answer: Because it gives Johnsy the strength to live.

questions.question: What is the turning point of the story?
questions.answer: When the last leaf remains on the creeper despite the storm.

questions.question: How does the story show the power of art?
questions.answer: Behrman’s painting inspires hope and saves a life.

questions.question: What is the central conflict of the story?
questions.answer: Johnsy’s struggle between despair and hope.

questions.question: Why is Behrman remembered as a hero?
questions.answer: Because he sacrificed his life to save Johnsy.

questions.question: How does the story build suspense?
questions.answer: Through the falling leaves and the stormy weather.

questions.question: What change occurs in Johnsy by the end?
questions.answer: She becomes hopeful and determined to live.

questions.question: Why is the title 'The Last Leaf' meaningful?
questions.answer: It represents hope and sacrifice.

questions.question: State the main moral of the story.
questions.answer: Hope and selfless sacrifice can save lives.
